req.title: Upload onboarding document
req.statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected.
preconditions: authenticated

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three equivalence partitions for file type (PDF/PNG/JPG vs. other), two size partitions (<=10MB vs. >10MB), and a critical boundary at exactly 10MB. EP covers valid/invalid MIME types and sizes. BVA targets the 10MB boundary (exactly 10MB, 10MB+1 byte). NEG covers oversized and disallowed MIME uploads. DT covers the combination of valid type + valid size producing success with virus-scan queued. STC covers the transition from 'unauthenticated' to 'authenticated' before upload is permitted. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up temp files.

tests.name: test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit
preconditions: User is authenticated and session token is valid
A valid PDF file of 1MB exists
Endpoint /documents/upload is reachable
actions: Create a 1MB in-memory PDF-like byte payload with correct MIME type
POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-WEB-077: valid PDF <= 10MB must be stored
R-WEB-077: virus scan must be queued on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

def test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit(auth_session, base_url):
    file_size = 1 * 1024 * 1024  # 1 MB
    payload = io.BytesIO(b"%PDF-1.4" + b"A" * (file_size - 8))
    files = {"file": ("onboarding.pdf", payload, "application/pdf")}
    resp = auth_session.post(f"{base_url}/documents/upload", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body, "document_id missing from response"
    assert body.get("virus_scan_queued") is True, "virus_scan_queued must be true"

tests.name: test_upload_valid_png_under_limit
preconditions: User is authenticated and session token is valid
A valid PNG file of 500KB exists
Endpoint /documents/upload is reachable
actions: Create a 500KB byte payload with image/png MIME type
POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-WEB-077: valid PNG <= 10MB must be stored
R-WEB-077: virus scan must be queued on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

def test_upload_valid_png_under_limit(auth_session, base_url):
    png_header = b"\x89PNG\r\n\x1a\n"
    file_size = 500 * 1024
    payload = io.BytesIO(png_header + b"B" * (file_size - len(png_header)))
    files = {"file": ("onboarding.png", payload, "image/png")}
    resp = auth_session.post(f"{base_url}/documents/upload", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body, "document_id missing from response"
    assert body.get("virus_scan_queued") is True, "virus_scan_queued must be true"

tests.name: test_upload_file_exceeds_10mb_returns_413
preconditions: User is authenticated and session token is valid
A PDF file of 10MB + 1 byte is prepared
Endpoint /documents/upload is reachable
actions: Create an in-memory byte payload of 10MB + 1 byte with PDF MIME type
POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with auth header
Capture HTTP response status
expected: Response body contains error message referencing size limit
trace: R-WEB-077: files > 10MB must be rejected with 413
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

def test_upload_file_exceeds_10mb_returns_413(auth_session, base_url):
    file_size = 10 * 1024 * 1024 + 1  # 10MB + 1 byte
    payload = io.BytesIO(b"%PDF-1.4" + b"X" * (file_size - 8))
    files = {"file": ("too_large.pdf", payload, "application/pdf")}
    resp = auth_session.post(f"{base_url}/documents/upload", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 413, f"Expected 413, got: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, "Error detail missing from 413 response"

tests.name: test_upload_disallowed_mime_type_returns_415
preconditions: User is authenticated and session token is valid
An EXE file of 100KB is prepared
Endpoint /documents/upload is reachable
actions: Create a 100KB payload with application/octet-stream MIME type
POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with auth header
Capture HTTP response status
expected: Response body contains error message referencing allowed formats
trace: R-WEB-077: files with MIME not in allow-list must be rejected with 415
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

def test_upload_disallowed_mime_type_returns_415(auth_session, base_url):
    file_size = 100 * 1024
    payload = io.BytesIO(b"MZ" + b"\x00" * (file_size - 2))  # EXE magic bytes
    files = {"file": ("malware.exe", payload, "application/octet-stream")}
    resp = auth_session.post(f"{base_url}/documents/upload", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 415, f"Expected 415, got: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, "Error detail missing from 415 response"

tests.name: test_upload_file_exactly_10mb_is_accepted
preconditions: User is authenticated and session token is valid
A PDF file of exactly 10485760 bytes (10MB) is prepared
Endpoint /documents/upload is reachable
actions: Create an in-memory byte payload of exactly 10 * 1024 * 1024 bytes with PDF MIME type
POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with auth header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 200 or 201 response (boundary value is within the allowed limit)
trace: R-WEB-077: upper boundary of 10MB must be accepted (inclusive)
BVA: test at max valid boundary point
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

def test_upload_file_exactly_10mb_is_accepted(auth_session, base_url):
    file_size = 10 * 1024 * 1024  # exactly 10MB
    payload = io.BytesIO(b"%PDF-1.4" + b"C" * (file_size - 8))
    files = {"file": ("boundary_exact.pdf", payload, "application/pdf")}
    resp = auth_session.post(f"{base_url}/documents/upload", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 2xx at 10MB boundary, got: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body, "document_id missing from response at boundary"
    assert body.get("virus_scan_queued") is True, "virus_scan_queued must be true at boundary"

tests.name: test_upload_rejected_when_not_authenticated
preconditions: No valid session token is present (unauthenticated state)
A valid 1MB PDF file is prepared
Endpoint /documents/upload is reachable
actions: Create a 1MB in-memory PDF payload
POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload WITHOUT authentication header
Capture HTTP response status
trace: R-WEB-077: precondition states actor must be authenticated
STC: transition from unauthenticated state must not reach upload success state
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io
import requests

def test_upload_rejected_when_not_authenticated(base_url):
    file_size = 1 * 1024 * 1024
    payload = io.BytesIO(b"%PDF-1.4" + b"D" * (file_size - 8))
    files = {"file": ("unauth_upload.pdf", payload, "application/pdf")}
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/documents/upload", files=files)  # no auth
    assert resp.status_code in (401, 403), f"Expected 401/403 without auth, got: {resp.status_code}"

tests.name: test_upload_decision_table_jpg_valid_size
preconditions: User is authenticated
A valid JPG file of 2MB is prepared
Endpoint /documents/upload is reachable
actions: Create a 2MB byte payload with image/jpeg MIME type and JFIF header
POST multipart/form-data to /documents/upload with auth header
Capture response status and body
expected: virus_scan_queued is true — DT row: MIME=JPG AND size<=10MB => ACCEPT
trace: R-WEB-077: JPG is in allow-list; file <= 10MB must succeed
DT: condition combination MIME=image/jpeg AND size=2MB => stored + scan queued
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

def test_upload_decision_table_jpg_valid_size(auth_session, base_url):
    jfif_header = b"\xff\xd8\xff\xe0"
    file_size = 2 * 1024 * 1024
    payload = io.BytesIO(jfif_header + b"E" * (file_size - len(jfif_header)))
    files = {"file": ("onboarding.jpg", payload, "image/jpeg")}
    resp = auth_session.post(f"{base_url}/documents/upload", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status for JPG upload: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body, "document_id missing"
    assert body.get("virus_scan_queued") is True, "virus_scan_queued must be true"
